response.justification: Bogdan is a capable generalist with strong TypeScript/Node.js and developer-tooling experience, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. Java appears only peripherally across his ~16-year career, Kotlin is entirely absent, and there is no evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework. While his work on the Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts library, and NI LabWindows CVI components shows genuine interest in developer tooling, this does not compensate for the absence of JVM depth and enterprise framework expertise that the role explicitly demands.
strengths: Genuine developer-tooling instinct demonstrated through building a D-language test runner (Trial), assertion library (Fluent-Asserts), and MCP code generators
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) consistently applied across multiple senior and staff-level roles
Breadth of system experience (web, mobile, embedded, micro-services) signals adaptability and architectural awareness
concerns: Primary technical identity is TypeScript/Node.js; Java is listed as a skill but appears only briefly for robotic-arm work and a short NI stint — no sustained, production Java experience is evident
Zero mention of Kotlin, Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework across the entire career history
No evidence of JVM internals knowledge, IDE plugin development, or experience with large-scale Java codebases — the three pillars of this role
